    Taco Bell Tests New Crispy Chicken Crunchwrap Sliders
    Taco Bell is testing new Crispy Chicken Crunchwrap Sliders for a limited time at participating locations in the Knoxville, TN area. Featuring a snack-sized version of Taco Bell's signature Crunchwrap, each one comes filled with crispy-fried chicken nuggets, a three-cheese blend, pico de gallo, and a choice of Creamy Chipotle or new Sriracha Chile Sauce.  Crispy Chicken Crunchwrap Sliders are priced at $2.49 each. Taco Bell has occasionally served up Crunchwrap Sliders since 2014. They were most recently offered in March of this year. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Nissin Releases New Turkey Dinner and Pumpkin Pie Cup Noodles Flavors
    Nissin gets into the Thanksgiving spirit this year with the launch of its new Turkey Dinner and Pumpkin Pie Cup Noodles flavors. The company describes the new flavors as a "moist" solution to the problems of "overcooked turkey, parched pumpkin pie crusts, or dry conversations." Turkey Dinner Cup Noodles features "rich essence of herb-seasoned turkey broth with sweet corn, tangy cranberry bursts, crisp green beans, warming spices, and tender noodles," while Pumpkin Pie offers a "satisfying pumpkin flavor blended with caramelized brown sugar sweetness and signature pie spices." The two new seasonal varieties mark the latest in a line of unexpected flavors from Cup Noodles. Others have included Dill Pickle, Campfire S'mores, Everything Bagel, and more. Turkey Dinner and Pumpkin Pie Cup Noodles are available now for a limited time exclusively at Walmart stores and the Walmart website. They're priced at $1.18 per cup. Photo via Nissin. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

  • Open

  • Open

    Carl's Jr. Tests New Tri-Tip Burger
    Carl's Jr. is testing a new Tri-Tip Burger at participating locations in the Denver area. The new burger features a charbroiled beef patty, sliced sirloin tri-tip, grilled onions, bistro steak sauce, and a slice of Swiss cheese, served on a toasted brioche bun. Double (two beef patties and two slices of Swiss cheese) and Triple (three patties and three slices of cheese) versions are also available. The Tri-Tip is definitely on the premium end of the pricing spectrum, starting at $9.99 for the Single version (compared to $6.49 for a Single Famous Star). Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Chick-fil-A Opens New Beverage Cafe Concept
    Chick-fil-A, under its Red Wagon Ventures subsidiary, has opened a new beverage cafe concept called "Daybright Coffee and Refreshment," featuring a "vibrant mix of crafted beverages and delightful bites that are made in-house daily." The new cafe features both a dining room and dual drive-thru lanes and is open Monday through Saturday, from 6AM to 9PM. It is located at 4326 Jimmy Lee Smith Parkway in Hiram, GA.  The Daybright menu includes: Coffee, including cold brew, protein coffees, and lattes with add-ons like cold foam and syrup. Iced teas and lemonades. Fizzes - Sparkling beverages made with fruit juices.  Smoothies Bright Pressed Juices - Cold-pressed bottled juice blends. Daily Donuts - Baked fresh daily with olive oil.  Portable food, including a warm sandwich, stuffed English muffins, a breakfast burrito, egg bites, and a chocolate pull-apart.  A kid’s menu that includes a PB&J, egg bites, and smoothies. The new concept follows on the heels of McDonald's recently shuttered CosMcs cafe concept and Taco Bell's expanding Live Mas Cafe. Photo via Chick-fil-A. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Shake Shack Offers $1 Soda, $3 Fries, and $5 Shakes
    Shake Shack offers a promotion where you can get soda for $1, classic crinkle-cut fries for $3, and regular menu shakes for $5 for a limited time. The deal is available when ordering through the Shake Shack website or mobile app. It specifically does not include limited-time shake flavors. Regular menu shake flavors include Vanilla, Chocolate, Strawberry, Cookies and Cream, Coffee, Black & White, and Chocolate & Vanilla. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Zaxbys Launches New Asian Zensation Menu
    Expanding on the popularity of its Asian Zensation Zalad (which was made a permanent menu item last year), Zaxbys launches its limited-time Asian Zensation menu, featuring the new Giant Asian Zensation Chicken Finger Wrap and Sweet Asian Glazed Chicken Finger Plate. The new Giant Asian Zensation Wrap basically takes the Asian Zensation Salad and wraps it up in a warm flour tortilla, i.e., it's a wrap that contains chopped hand-breaded Fried Chicken Fingerz or grilled chicken, mixed greens, carrots, red cabbage, wonton strips, Asian Slaw, and a drizzle of Teriyaki Sauce. The wrap comes with Crinkle Fries and a small drink. The new Sweet Asian Glazed Chicken Finger Plate includes four, five, or six Chicken Fingerz tossed in a Sweet Asian Glaze, served with coleslaw, Texas toast, Crinkle Fries, and a small drink. While they don't launch nationally until Monday, November 3, 2025, the new menu items are available now when ordering through the Zaxbys website and mobile app, as well as through third-party delivery platforms. Additionally, the chain will be adding a new Cookie Butter Milkshake to the menu. Zaxbys' first-ever holiday shake features a vanilla shake blended with cinnamon-spiced syrup and cookie pieces, topped with whipped cream, cookie crumbles, and a maraschino cherry. Photo via Zaxbys. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

Through October 31, purchasing a Halloween Bucket earns you a $5 Reward for your next visit.  - Baskin-Robbins - Through October 31, Baskin-Robbins Rewards members get 20% off the purchase of a Polar Pizza, plus 31% off all scoops on Halloween.   • Open

    $3.99 King Jr. Meals at Burger King Through November 2, 2025
    Burger King is offering a limited-time deal where you can pick up its King Jr. kid's meals for $3.99 each when ordering through the Burger King app or website through Sunday, November 2, 2025. The deal is good on a Cheeseburger, Hamburger, or 4-piece Chicken Nuggets King Jr. Meal. The King Jr. Meal includes an entree, a side (value-size French fries, Mott's Applesauce, value-size Onion Rings, or value-size Have-sies), an Honest Kids Apple Juice (or milk), and a toy (that can be swapped for a chocolate chip cookie). Photo via Burger King. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Carl's Jr. Introduces New Cali XL and Churro Shake
    Carl's Jr. introduces the new Cali XL burger and new Churro Hand-Scooped Ice-Cream Shake at participating locations chain-wide. The Cali XL is a larger version of the fast-food chain's value-priced California Classic Double Cheeseburger. It features two charbroiled beef patties, two slices of American cheese, grilled onions, Classic Sauce, lettuce, and tomato on a toasted sesame seed bun. It's priced at $5.99 with the tagline, "Stacked Tall. Priced Small." In comparison, the similarly appointed California Classic Double Cheeseburger comes with smaller beef patties, only has one slice of American cheese, and is served on a plain bun for $4.99. The new Churro Hand-Scooped Ice-Cream Shake consists of vanilla ice cream blended with churro syrup, and churro crumbles, crowned with whipped topping and more churro crumbles. Prices vary, but it costs $6.19 at my local Carl's Jr. here in Los Angeles. Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Jack-O Pizza is Back at Papa Murphy's for Halloween 2025
    Take-and-bake pizza chain Papa Murphy's brings back its Jack-O Pizza for the 2025 Halloween season. The jack-o-lantern-shaped, take-and-bake pizza starts at $10 for a 14-inch large (it's $13 for a 16-inch family size) and comes topped with red sauce, whole-milk mozzarella, cheddar, and a pepperoni face. You can also get a large Jack-O-Pizza and Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough for $15. Papa Murphy's calls it a "Scream of a Deal." The Jack-O Pizza is available for a limited time through November 2, 2025. Photo via Papa Murphy's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Fried Strawberry Cheesecake Bites Return to Checkers and Rally's
    Checkers and Rally's have brought back Fried Strawberry Cheesecake Bites for a limited time. The snackable dessert features bite-sized, crispy pastry shells filled with strawberry pieces and vanilla cheesecake and dusted with powdered sugar. Prices vary, but an order of four Fried Strawberry Cheesecake Bites costs $2.99 here in Los Angeles. Photo via Checkers and Rally's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Taco Bell Tests New Diablo Nuggets
    Taco Bell is testing a spicy version of its chicken nuggets, called "Diablo Nuggets," for a limited time at participating locations in the Minneapolis area. They've also brought back Loaded Taco Topped Fries for a limited time in the same area. Diablo Nuggets feature the same white-meat chicken and tortilla-chip breading as the chain's Crispy Chicken Nuggets, only they're dusted with a Diablo Sauce-flavored seasoning for "an extra fiery kick." The new nuggets are available a la carte in a five-piece order for $4.99 and a 10-piece order for $7.99. You can also get a five-piece Diablo Nuggets combo (with fries and a medium fountain drink) for $6.99 and a 10-piece combo for $9.99 Additionally, there's a Nuggets Deluxe Meal and a $9 Discovery Luxe Box that includes Diablo Nuggets. The Nuggets Deluxe Meal includes five classic or Diablo Nuggets, a Seasoned Beef Chalupa, a Crunchy Taco, regular Nacho Fries, and a large fountain drink for $11.49. The $9 Discovery Luxe Box is a box combo that includes five classic or Diablo Nuggets, regular Nacho Fries, a Doritos Locos Taco, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink. Loaded Taco Topped Fries (here's my previous review from 2021) cost $4.99 and feature seasoned fries topped with seasoned beef, shredded lettuce, Spicy Ranch, fiesta strips (crispy red tortilla strips), diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, and warm nacho cheese sauce. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Deep Fried Turkey is Back at Arby's Along with New Apple Pie Shake
    Arby's celebrates Thanksgiving this year with the return of Deep Fried Turkey sandwiches and the debut of its new Apple Pie Shake. Available for a limited time now, Arby's Deep Fried Turkey lineup includes two options: - Deep Fried Turkey Gobbler Sandwich (pictured) - A quarter-pound of thick-sliced deep-fried turkey, crispy-fried onions, melted Swiss cheese, stuffing, cranberry spread, and mayo on a toasted sub roll. The seasonal sandwich got some tweaks this year compared to last year's version, which featured a savory herb spread and everything seasoning. The addition of stuffing is new for any Arby's sandwich, and previous renditions came on a round bun. - Deep Fried Turkey Club Sandwich - A quarter pound of thick-sliced deep-fried Turkey served with bacon, cheddar cheese, tomato, lettuce, and mayo, on a toasted sub roll.  The new Apple Pie Shake blends the flavor of baked green apples and warming spices (i.e., apple pie-flavored syrup) into Arby's vanilla shake base and is topped with whipped cream and a dusting of cinnamon sugar. Prices for the Thanksgiving-inspired menu items vary, but the Deep-Fried Turkey Gobbler costs $6.99 at an Arby's here in Los Angeles, the Deep Fried Turkey Club is $6.69, and a regular-size Apple Pie Shake goes for $3.89. Photo via Arby's. Nutritional Info - Arby's Deep Fried Turkey Gobbler Sandwich (311g) Calories - 690 Fat - 28g (Saturated Fat - 10g) Sodium - 2130mg Carbs - 74g (Sugar - 14g) Protein - 36g Nutritional Info - Arby's Deep Fried Turkey Club Sandwich (301g) Calories - 650 Fat - 35g (Saturated Fat - 10g) Sodium - 2030mg Carbs - 46g (Sugar - 6g) Protein - 40g Nutritional Info - Arby's Apple Pie Shake - Regular (377g) Calories - 560 Fat - 16g (Saturated Fat - 10g) Sodium - 290mg Carbs - 94g (Sugar - 82g) Protein - 11g Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

  • Open

    Thoughts for Food: Have You Ever Gone to a Fast Food Restaurant for a Toy?
    The other night, as I was sitting in the drive-thru lane at McDonald's to redeem a free Boo Bucket Happy Meal with my rewards points, I recalled the times as a kid when I'd beg my dad to go to McDonald's to get a Happy Meal because of a toy I saw on TV. Then, I thought about how I don't really see my nieces and nephews doing the same. Interestingly, the few times I've seen someone ask for a Happy Meal toy recently, it was an adult trying to get a specific toy (to complete a set, I guess?). So my question for you guys is, "Have you ever gone to a fast food restaurant primarily to get a toy or other merch (like Coca-Cola glasses or Halloween buckets and the like)?" If you're older, do you feel there's a nostalgia aspect that drives the purchase? Saturday morning cartoons and the Happy Meal commercials that came with had more than a little to do with my want for Happy Meal toys. But that's not something that exists anymore. There's a generational line after which a person did not grow up with Saturday morning cartoons and instead grew up watching YouTube and various on-demand streaming services. On another note, how do you hear about Happy Meal toys or other kids' meal toys? I learn about them through writing this blog (for over 10 years now!) and from point-of-sale displays when I'm at a restaurant, but I'm curious to learn how it is for others. As for the Boo Bucket, I plan to fill it with candy and have my nieces and nephews play "Guess how much this cost me?" for it (and then making them share the candy with their siblings anyway...). Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Crumbl Bakes New Halloween Confetti Milkshake Cookie and More Through November 1, 2025
    With Halloween coming up this week, Crumbl welcomes a weekly lineup that includes the new Halloween Confetti Milkshake Cookie, new Chocolate Peanut Butter Brownie featuring Reese's Pieces, and more. Here are the baked desserts you'll find as part of the limited-time selection at Crumbl from October 27 to November 1, 2025: - Halloween Confetti Milkshake Cookie - A chilled confetti sugar cookie rolled in white, orange, and black sprinkles and topped with cake-flavored buttercream and a whipped cream rosette. - Chocolate Peanut Butter Brownie featuring Reese's Pieces - Afudge brownie packed with chopped Reese's Pieces, topped with peanut butter frosting, more chopped Reese's Pieces, and a drizzle of milk chocolate.  - Dirt Cake Cookie - A warm dark chocolate cookie coated in cookies & cream crumbs, topped with fudge frosting, chocolate streusel, and a gummy worm. - Cookie Dough Cookie - A chilled brown sugar cookie topped with cookie dough buttercream and cookie dough chunks. - Pumpkin Cheesecake Cookie - A chilled buttery graham cookie topped with pumpkin cheesecake frosting and graham cracker streusel. - French Toast Cookie - A buttery toast-shaped cookie topped with a cinnamon egg wash, a dollop of buttercream, a drizzle of syrup, and a dusting of powdered sugar. - Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt. In addition to the listed cookie, Crumbl is celebrating Halloween with a Graveyard Menu this week. It's a variation of the chain's recent "Secret Menu," where each location will offer a Graveyard flavor that is "making a comeback after years in the Crumbl afterlife." Like the Secret Menu, you can check the Crumbl website and mobile app to see what's available near you. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

  • Open

    McDonald's Introduces New Buffalo Ranch and New Chips Ahoy! Frappe
    McDonald's has released its new, limited-time Buffalo Ranch and new, limited-time Chips Ahoy! Frappe. While the official launch isn't until October 28, 2025, some locations are already serving the new sauce and drink. McDonald's Buffalo Ranch is available as a dipping sauce and is featured in the Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy sandwich and Buffalo Ranch Snack Wrap. The Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy is available in a regular and Deluxe version. Here's what's in each version of the sandwich: - Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy - A McCrispy chicken filet, Buffalo Ranch, applewood-smoked bacon, and crinkle-cut pickles, served on a toasted potato roll. It costs $6.59 near me in Los Angeles. - Bacon Buffalo Ranch Deluxe McCrispy - A McCrispy chicken filet, Buffalo Ranch sauce, applewood-smoked bacon, lettuce, and tomato, served on a toasted potato roll. The sandwich is priced at $7.19 near me. The Buffalo Ranch Snack Wrap features a McCrispy Strip, Buffalo Ranch sauce, shredded cheese, and shredded lettuce, wrapped in a soft flour tortilla (i.e., it's the Ranch Snack Wrap, but with Buffalo Ranch sauce instead of Ranch). It's priced at $2.99. If the trio seems familiar, it's the same menu as for the outgoing Special Edition Gold Sauce, only with Buffalo Ranch. You can also get a dipping cup of the new sauce with an order of McCrispy Strips or Chicken McNuggets. You can also purchase the sauce cup separately (prices vary, but it costs $0.29 near me). The new Chips Ahoy! Frappe is a coffee frappe blended with chocolate chip cookie syrup and Chips Ahoy! cookie pieces, topped with whipped cream and more Chips Ahoy! cookie pieces. Prices vary, but a small Chips Ahoy! Frappe costs $3.39 here in Los Angeles. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

  • Open

    Taco Bell Tests New Gordita Crunch Sliders
    Taco Bell is testing new Gordita Crunch Sliders for a limited time at participating locations in the Nashville area. The new menu item features a set of two snack-sized pillowy flatbreads folded around crispy white-corn shells with a layer of melted three-cheese blend to hold them together. Nestled inside the shells is a filling of your choice of seasoned beef, slow-roasted chicken, or grilled, marinated steak, plus Creamy Chipotle sauce and pico de gallo. They range from $4.49 to $5.49 (varying with location) for the chicken or steak versions and $3.49 for the seasoned beef version. While essentially a mini rendition of the Cheesy Gordita Crunch, the Gordita Crunch Sliders sport a slightly different taco shell (made of white corn instead of yellow corn) and a different combination of fillings (the Cheesy Gordita Crunch is filled with seasoned beef, shredded lettuce, shredded cheddar cheese, and Spicy Ranch). Gordita Crunch Sliders are also available as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box and a $6.99 Snack Trio, which is an in-app-only item available at select locations. The $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box includes a set of two Gordita Crunch Sliders, a Nacho Cheese Doritos Locos Taco, a Crunchy Taco, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink. The $6.99 Snack Trio includes a set of two Gordita Crunch Sliders, Nacho Fries with nacho cheese sauce, and two Cinnabon Delights. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    New Cookie Butter Chips Ahoy! Cookies Available Now
    Nabisco launches new Cookie Butter-inspired Chewy Chips Ahoy! cookies ahead of the 2025 holiday season. The limited-edition flavor features the brand's signature soft, chewy cookie with white fudge chips and a creamy cookie butter-flavored filling. You can find Cookie Butter-inspired Chewy Chips Ahoy! now at retailers nationwide for a limited time. They come in a 9.9-oz package. Prices vary, but my local Walmart here in Los Angeles sells them for $4.78. That's a premium price, considering a 13.3-oz package of regular Chewy Chips Ahoy costs $3.28 at the same store. However, it's comparable in price to the limited-edition Stranger Things Chips Ahoy! cookies. Photo via Chips Ahoy!. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    Checkers & Rally's Tests Two New Spicy Burgers
    Checkers & Rally's are testing two new spicy burgers, the Hatch Canyon Burger and Snake Bite Burger, for a limited time at select locations in Southern California and Arizona. The Hatch Canyon Burger (pictured) features a large beef patty, American cheese, hatch green chiles, crispy-fried onions, and Secret Sauce, served on a toasted, bakery-style bun. Prices vary, but the burger costs $4.99 at the nearest Rally's in Los Angeles. The Snake Bite Burger features a large beef patty, American cheese, sauteed jalapenos & onions, crispy-fried onions, and Secret Sauce, served on a toasted, bakery-style bun. If you're following along, the only difference between the two spicy burgers is that one has hatch green chiles, while the other contains sauteed jalapenos and onions. Like the Hatch Canyon Burger, the Snake Bite Burger costs $4.99. Photo via Checkers & Rally's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Sonic Offers Weekly "Spooky Savings" Deals Through November 2, 2025
    In celebration of Halloween, Sonic is offering a series of weekly "Spooky Savings" deals exclusively through the Sonic website or mobile app until Sunday, November 2, 2025. The schedule of "Spooky Savings" deals goes as follows: Through October 26, 2025: Free Medium Tots or Groovy Fries with purchase. Free Small Classic Shake with purchase. Free Sonic Cheeseburger with purchase. Free Small Premium Chicken Bites with purchase. Through November 2, 2025: Free Entree with purchase (not available on October 31, 2025) Free Medium Slush with purchase. Free Trick or Treat Blast with purchase. Free Small Snack or Side with purchase. In addition to the weekly deals, Sonic will again be selling corn dogs for 50 cents each on Halloween, Friday, October 31, 2025, at participating locations nationwide. Photo via Sonic. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
